title: Ageratina pomaderrifolia
taxonomy: Ageratina pomaderrifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Ageratina, which is a group of flowering plants known as shrubby plants or shrubs. The genus Ageratina is part of the tribe Eupatorieae, which is a group of plants that are commonly found in the Americas. The Eupatorieae tribe is part of the subfamily Asteroideae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Ageratina pomaderrifolia is a member of this complex classification system, with its full classification being Ageratina pomaderrifolia, Eupatorieae, Asteroideae, Asteraceae.

intro: Ageratina pomaderrifolia, also known as Mexican coleus or Mexican ageratina, is a shrubby perennial plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant is valued for its health benefits, particularly in traditional medicine, as it is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ageratina pomaderrifolia include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ageratina pomaderrifolia is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into teas, syrups, and salves, to treat various health conditions.
